channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How to spot red flags in a new romantic relationship
item.description: Late-night research by a friend revealed how rushing into romance can lead to risky situations. Anne had just met a man who seemed very charming, but she felt uneasy because he was moving too quickly,...

item.title: European Businesses Pledge Continued Investment and Partnership in Tanzania
item.description: European investors don promise to continue strengthening their trade and investment links with Tanzania. The statement was made during the 10-year anniversary of the European Business Group Tanzania (...

item.title: Rockies score seven in sixth inning to defeat Blue Jays 14-5
item.description: The Colorado Rockies defeated the Toronto Blue Jays 14-5 on Monday, highlighted by a seven-run sixth inning. Troy Johnston hit a two-run homer that sparked the big inning, followed by Braxton Fulford'...

item.title: Investigation Underway Into Diepsloot Teenager’s Fatal Shooting
item.description: The death of 16-year-old Thabani Mazibuko in Diepsloot has sparked grief and protests within the community. According to witnesses, Mazibuko was shot by a police officer after he attempted to interven...

item.title: Elderly veteran denied compensation after abuse over sexuality during military service
item.description: Michael Pitchford, a 78-year-old former serviceman from Rottingdean, has claimed the Ministry of Defence (MoD) denied his request for financial reparations despite the severe mistreatment he endured f...

item.title: TVK Chief Vijay Faces Case for Election Code Violation in Peravallur
item.description: In Tamil Nadu, the police have registered a case against Vijay, the chief of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), for alleged violations of the Model Code of Conduct. The complaint, lodged by Flying Squad ...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Heavy Rain in Several Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a forecast for the next 24 hours warning of increased rainfall in certain parts of the country. Between 9 pm on Monday, March 30, and 9 pm on Tuesd...

item.title: AG Challenges Legality of TLS Providing Free Legal Aid in Kigoma Election Petition
item.description: The Attorney General (AG) of Tanzania has raised legal concerns over the involvement of the Tanganyika Law Society (TLS) in providing free legal assistance to petitioners contesting the election of Ki...

item.title: Court dismisses former presidents’ attempt to remove Justice Khampepe on procedural grounds
item.description: The high court has dismissed former presidents Jacob Zuma and Thabo Mbeki’s application to remove retired Justice Sisi Khampepe from leading the Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) cases inquiry...

item.title: Pakistan and China Discuss Framework for Potential US-Iran Dialogue
item.description: Pakistan and China are holding high-level talks in Beijing to fine-tune a potential framework for dialogue between Iran and the United States aimed at ending the ongoing war in the region. Deputy Prim...

item.title: Honouring Rhoda Roberts AO and Her Enduring Connection to Country
item.description: This article from NITV pays heartfelt tribute to Aunty Rhoda Roberts AO, a trailblazing Bundjalung woman whose life’s work celebrated and strengthened the cultural fabric of First Nations peoples acro...

item.title: Internal conflicts raise possibility of federal intervention in Victorian Liberal Party
item.description: The Victorian Liberal Party is currently experiencing significant internal turmoil following the controversial removal of MP Moira Deeming from party support for her Upper House seat. Party leader Jes...

item.title: Sooke student charged after online contact with U.S. teen accused of making school shooting threats
item.description: A youth from Sooke, British Columbia, has been arrested and charged after police uncovered online conversations with a U.S. teenager who allegedly made threats of a school shooting. The RCMP announced...

item.title: Pendo Bigambo’s work in improving post-harvest systems and supporting women farmers
item.description: Pendo Bigambo imekuwa ikichangia sana katika kilimo Tanzania kwa zaidi ya miaka 20, hasa katika kuhakikisha chakula hakipotei baada ya mavuno na kwamba wakulima wanapata kipato thabiti. Kazi yake inas...

item.title: Kenyans urged to actively join national budget talks to strengthen health and accountability
item.description: The article by Karambu Muthaura, an excise taxes expert and health taxes consultant, stresses the importance of public engagement in Kenya’s national budget process. She explains that citizen particip...

item.title: Empowering Women Entrepreneurs to Boost East Africa’s Economy
item.description: In East Africa, women are the backbone of the economy, particularly in Tanzania and Kenya. They own a large portion of micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) and form the majority of the agricul...

item.title: DOJ and PAGCOR sign data-sharing deal to enforce gambling ban on state workers
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has entered into a data-sharing agreement with the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) to help enforce the government’s prohibition on gambling among p...

item.title: Tensions Escalate in Middle East as US Threatens Iran's Energy Infrastructure
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated after Iran rejected peace proposals from the United States, describing them as 'unrealistic, illogical and excessive.' The conflict intensified following wav...

item.title: APC Chieftain Says Kwankwaso’s Exit Confirms NNPP Is No Longer Viable
item.description: Mohammed Jamo, one of the chieftains of the All Progressives Congress (APC), don yarn say the recent political moves, especially Musa Kwankwaso’s shift from the New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP) to the...

item.title: Lagos Court Adjourns Badejo Case After Long Delays
item.description: On Monday, the Federal High Court in Ikoyi, Lagos, was meant to hear the case of UK-based Nigerian blogger and activist, Maureen Omorinola Badejo. The session wey suppose start by 9:00 a.m. dey keep d...
